title: Anisomeris malaneoides
taxonomy: Anisomeris malaneoides is classified in the family Capparaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as caper family. This family is part of the larger order Brassicales, which includes various flowering plants such as cabbages, mustards, and shepherds purse. The order Brassicales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants that have two cotyledons in their seeds. Anisomeris malaneoides is a unique species found in the Americas, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Brassicales order. The classification of Anisomeris malaneoides is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics, which are used to determine its place within the plant kingdom.
uses: Anisomeris malaneoides, also known as the malane or velvet flower, is a plant species with various medicinal uses. Its roots and leaves are traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
